page: Amazon Web Services c6in.8xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz, delivering 32 virtual CPUs across 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It utilizes the Nitro hypervisor and includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration flags like AES, VAES, and SHA_NI.

This instance does not include local NVMe SSD storage, requiring reliance on network-attached Elastic Block Store volumes. However, it is network-optimized, offering a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. The lack of local storage represents a tradeoff for workloads requiring high-speed local scratch space, though the high network throughput mitigates data transfer bottlenecks to external storage.

Benchmark data places the c6in.8xlarge in the average performance tier across multiple tests. It achieves a Geekbench multi-core score of 14,044.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 36,688.86. Memory bandwidth tests show 147.72 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 66.69 GB/sec for large block write operations. In database operations, the instance handles 31.1 million Redis SET operations per second. For machine learning, it processes 135M parameter LLM prompts at 1824.74 tokens/second, but performance drops significantly on larger models, yielding a weak 5.83 tokens/second for 70B parameter prompt processing. This indicates a qualitative cost-efficiency tradeoff where the instance is highly capable for medium-scale compute tasks but inefficient for large-scale AI model execution.

The c6in.8xlarge is suited for network-intensive compute workloads, software compilation, medium-scale database operations, and web serving. Its high network bandwidth and dedicated compute cores support distributed computing, static web serving, and moderate-sized serialization or compression tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c6in.8xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance featuring a dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores running at 3.5 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing 2.0 GB per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage but features a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks place the server in the average tier, with a Passmark CPU score of 36,688.86 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 14,044.0. It demonstrates strong capabilities in network-heavy tasks and medium-scale database operations, processing 31.1 million Redis SET operations per second. However, it shows weak performance for large-scale LLM inference. This instance is best suited for network-intensive compute workloads, software compilation, and web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6in.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR4 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, suited for network-intensive workloads and software compilation.

tagline: A network-optimized compute instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, 50 Gbps bandwidth, and balanced memory-to-core ratios for distributed workloads.

64.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 2.0 GB per core
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor optimization
No local storage, requiring network-attached EBS volumes
